perturbation.original_text: MediaLinx will not have access to the HCI Personal Health Records (being the longitudinal repository of personal health information about users of the Co-Branded Site) during or after the Term of this Agreement. During the Term of this Agreement and for a period of six (6) months thereafter, MediaLinx shall not use or publicly disclose the data collected from users of the Co-Branded Site if such use is for the purpose of providing health information, advertisements, or products that compete with those that reside on the Co-Branded Site. In other instances, MediaLinx shall be entitled to use or publicly disclose the data collected subject to privacy guidelines noted above. In addition to the foregoing, HCI shall ensure that the Co-Branded Site is integrated into the Sympatico site-wide registry in order for users to have a single login access for all Sympatico.ca and Sympatico co-branded web sites.
perturbation.changed_text: MediaLinx will have full access to the HCI Personal Health Records (being the longitudinal repository of personal health information about users of the Co-Branded Site) during and after the Term of this Agreement. During the Term of this Agreement and for a period of six (6) months thereafter, MediaLinx shall not use or publicly disclose the data collected from users of the Co-Branded Site if such use is for the purpose of providing health information, advertisements, or products that compete with those that reside on the Co-Branded Site. In other instances, MediaLinx shall be entitled to use or publicly disclose the data collected subject to privacy guidelines noted above. In addition to the foregoing, HCI shall ensure that the Co-Branded Site is integrated into the Sympatico site-wide registry in order for users to have a single login access for all Sympatico.ca and Sympatico co-branded web sites.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "will not have access" to "will have full access", MediaLinx's access to HCI Personal Health Records is now contradictory to the following sentence that restricts MLX from using the data collected from users of the Co-Branded Site for certain purposes for six months after the end of the agreement, which implies limited access rather than 'full access', creating an ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any and all customer data that is collected from the Co-Branded Site shall be collected and used in accordance with Canadian federal and provincial laws and U.S. Federal and State laws and both MediaLinx and HCI privacy guidelines and MediaLinx and HCI shall each own an undivided interest in such data. MediaLinx will have full access to the HCI Personal Health Records (being the longitudinal repository of personal health information about users of the Co-Branded Site) during and after the Term of this Agreement. During the Term of this Agreement and for a period of six (6) months thereafter, MediaLinx shall not use or publicly disclose the data collected from users of the Co-Branded Site if such use is for the purpose of providing health information, advertisements, or products that compete with those that reside on the Co-Branded Site. In other instances, MediaLinx shall be entitled to use or publicly disclose the data collected subject to privacy guidelines noted above. In addition to the foregoing, HCI shall ensure that the Co-Branded Site is integrated into the Sympatico site-wide registry in order for users to have a single login access for all Sympatico.ca and Sympatico co-branded web sites.
perturbation.justification: The change from "will not have access" to "will have full access" in relation to HCI Personal Health Records directly contradicts the subsequent clause that restricts MediaLinx from using the data collected from users of the Co-Branded Site for certain purposes for six months after the end of the agreement. The "full access" clause implies unrestricted use, whereas the restriction clause implies limited use. This creates an ambiguity and therefore an in-text contradiction, as 'full access' logically cannot exist alongside restricted use for a defined period. This adheres to the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: MLX will use all commercially reasonable efforts to maintain the user traffic at a monthly minimum of: . [*] page views three (3) months after the launch of the Co-Branded Site; . [*] page views six (6) months after the launch of the Co-Branded Site; and . [*] page views twelve (12) months after the launch of the Co-Branded Site.
perturbation.changed_text: MLX guarantees to maintain the user traffic at a monthly minimum of: . [*] page views three (3) months after the launch of the Co-Branded Site; . [*] page views six (6) months after the launch of the Co-Branded Site; and . [*] page views twelve (12) months after the launch of the Co-Branded Site.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'MLX will use all commercially reasonable efforts' to 'MLX guarantees to maintain' introduces an in-text contradiction in section 2f: ensure that in the event that user traffic to the Co-Branded Site falls below the levels outlined in Schedule 2, that MediaLinx will use all commercially reasonable efforts to increase promotion to the Co-Branded Site until usage to the site increases to levels above the levels outlined in Schedule 2; and', if MLX 'guarantees' a user traffic number there is no need to make 'commercially reasonable efforts to increase promotion', creating conflicting obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ensure that in the event that user traffic to the Co-Branded Site falls below the levels outlined in Schedule 2, that MediaLinx will use all commercially reasonable efforts to increase promotion to the Co-Branded Site until usage to the site increases to levels above the levels outlined in Schedule 2; and
perturbation.justification: The original text states that MLX will use 'commercially reasonable efforts' to maintain user traffic. The changed text states that MLX 'guarantees' to maintain user traffic. The contradicted text states that 'in the event that user traffic to the Co-Branded Site falls below the levels outlined in Schedule 2, that MediaLinx will use all commercially reasonable efforts to increase promotion to the Co-Branded Site'. A guarantee implies a stricter obligation than using commercially reasonable efforts. The changed text, therefore, contradicts the contradicted text as the 'commercially reasonable efforts' clause becomes superfluous and creates ambiguity around the actual obligation. The change creates a direct conflict regarding the level of commitment required from MLX.

perturbation.original_text: The effective date of the start of the Term will be from the Launch Date.
perturbation.changed_text: The effective date of the start of the Term will be July 1st, 1999.
perturbation.explanation: By specifying a fixed start date of July 1st, 1999, instead of tying it to the 'Launch Date', this creates a contradiction with section 1b, which states 'The Parties have agreed that they will mutually approve the design of the Co-Branded Site and that the launch date of the Co-Branded Site shall be September 1st, 1999 unless mutually extended by the Parties as agreed to in writing'. This inconsistency in the contract about when it will become effective can create uncertainty in enforcement
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties have agreed that they will mutually approve the design of the Co-Branded Site and that the launch date of the Co-Branded Site shall be September 1st, 1999 unless mutually extended by the Parties as agreed to in writing.
perturbation.justification: The changed text specifies a fixed start date of July 1st, 1999, while the contradicted text states that the launch date, and therefore the effective date, is September 1st, 1999, unless mutually extended. This creates a direct conflict regarding when the contract becomes effective, leading to uncertainty and potential unenforceability. As per Perturbation Explanation, the 'inconsistency in the contract about when it will become effective can create uncertainty in enforcement'.
